title: Useful thinking tools: insights from design and teaching
summary: Exploring practical thinking tools and frameworks from both design and education to enhance problem-solving and creativity.
content:
I stumbled across a great article by Scott H Young, "Twenty-Five Useful Thinking Tools". Young's 2018 article delves into various professions and the unique thinking tools they offer. Check it out via the link below, I wanted to highlight two insightful points from Young's article relevant to my roles and experience:

### 1. Communicating through design

In UX design, the principle that "the things you make communicate for you" is crucial. This idea, inspired by Don Norman's *The Design of Everyday Things*, highlights how well-designed products intuitively guide users on how to use them. For example, a well-crafted user interface (UI) should naturally lead users through a process without the need for detailed instructions. Similarly, in learning design, creating educational materials that intuitively guide learners through content can enhance understanding and retention. By designing systems and content that communicate their purpose and use seamlessly, we can significantly improve user and learner experiences.

### 2. Seeing through the eyes of a novice

In both UX and learning design, understanding the perspective of a novice is essential. This tool involves empathising with users or learners who are unfamiliar with the content or system. Effective designers and educators need to identify gaps in knowledge and craft experiences that bridge these gaps.

In UX, this means designing interfaces that are user-friendly and accessible to those with no prior experience.
In learning design, this might involve creating instructional materials that start from the basics and build up complexity in a logical, comprehensible manner. This scaffolding approach ensures that both users and learners are guided effectively from their current understanding to the desired outcome.

For me, these thinking tools from design and teaching can impact how we approach problem-solving and communication in UX and learning design. I highly recommend giving the article a read!

[Read the full article here](https://lnkd.in/gjf3srjF)

#UXDesign #LearningDesign #DesignThinking #EffectiveCommunication #ProblemSolving #LearningAndDevelopment
author: Rich Bartlett

title: Welcome to DesignToSolve!
summary: This blog aims to offer a nuanced approach to design thinking, exploring the delicate balance between empirical data, human-centric empathy, and intuitive decision-making.
content: This blog aims to offer a nuanced approach to design thinking, exploring the delicate balance between empirical data, human-centric empathy, and intuitive decision-making. Whether you're an academic looking to integrate design principles into educational settings or a UX newcomer navigating the complexities of the field, DesignToSolve serves as a resource for continuous learning and dialogue. Designed for both seasoned professionals and those new to the field, the blog covers a wide range of topics that address both the technical and emotional aspects of design. Dive in to enrich your understanding and engage in conversations that aim to help shape the future of design.
author: Rich Bartlett
